page: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge is a graphics-intensive virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores. The system includes 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance is equipped with a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU featuring 96 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with the Geekbench single-core score and bzip3 single-threaded decompression reaching top-tier status in the top 10% category. Multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range. The hardware excels in large language model inference tasks; prompt processing and text generation for small and medium models rank in the top tier. Prompt processing for large models is also strong, placing in the top 25%, though text generation data for the 70B model is unavailable.

The instance offers high resource density, combining a modern Blackwell-generation GPU with a substantial 96 GB VRAM allocation and fast local NVMe storage. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring large GPU memory footprints relative to compute cores, though the 8 vCPU limit may act as a bottleneck for highly parallel CPU-bound tasks. The server is suited for graphics-intensive applications, machine learning inference, LLM prompt processing, and database workloads requiring high-speed local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge is an x86_64 virtual machine instance optimized for graphics-intensive and GPU-accelerated workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8559C processor, 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD. The primary hardware accelerator is a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in single-core tasks, text processing, and LLM inference for small and medium models, while multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth tests fall in the average range. The high VRAM-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy graphics and machine learning tasks, though the 8-vCPU limit may constrain highly parallel CPU workloads. This instance is suited for LLM inference, graphics rendering, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge is an x86_64 instance featuring an Intel Xeon CPU, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM for accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA Blackwell GPU with 96 GB VRAM, DDR5 memory, and fast local NVMe storage.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM
8 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processor
64.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
1900 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM inference and single-core tasks
Suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database workloads
